title: British American Tobacco Announces Large Job Cuts in Technology Shift
subtitle: The tobacco giant aims to save millions by moving toward digital tools and away from traditional cigarettes.

summary.en: British American Tobacco is cutting 9,000 jobs to lower costs and focus on new technology. The company plans to remove 5,500 roles and move 3,500 more to outside companies. This change is part of a plan to save £600 million every year by 2028. Traditional cigarette sales are falling as more people choose vapes and nicotine pouches. The company says it will support workers through these difficult changes.
summary.zh: 英美菸草公司計畫裁減 9,000 個職位以降低成本並專注於新科技。該公司計畫裁撤 5,500 個職位，並將另外 3,500 個職位外包。此變革是 2028 年前每年節省 6 億英鎊計畫的一部分。隨著越來越多人選擇電子菸與尼古丁袋，傳統香菸銷量正持續下滑。公司表示將在這些艱難的變革中為員工提供支持。

paragraphs.en: British American Tobacco (BAT), one of the world’s largest tobacco companies, has announced a major plan to cut 9,000 jobs. This move represents nearly one-fifth of its total global workforce of 47,000 people. The company, which produces well-known cigarette brands like Lucky Strike and Dunhill, is making these changes to lower costs and become a more digital, technology-focused business.
paragraphs.zh: 全球最大的菸草公司之一英美菸草公司（British American Tobacco，簡稱 BAT）宣布了一項裁減 9,000 個職位的重大計畫。此舉約占其全球 4 萬 7,000 名員工總數的五分之一。該公司生產如「好彩」（Lucky Strike）和「登喜路」（Dunhill）等知名香菸品牌，此次變革旨在降低成本，並轉型為更數位化、以科技為導向的企業。

paragraphs.en: According to the BBC, the plan includes removing 5,500 roles completely and moving 3,500 other jobs to outside companies. This process, known as outsourcing, is part of a larger effort to save the company £600 million annually by 2028. The company confirmed that these cuts have already started and are expected to be finished by the end of this year. Importantly, the company noted that its operations in the United States will not be affected by these job losses.
paragraphs.zh: 根據 BBC 報導，該計畫包括徹底裁撤 5,500 個職位，並將另外 3,500 個職位外包給外部公司。這種稱為「外包」的流程，是該公司力求在 2028 年前每年節省 6 億英鎊計畫的一部分。公司證實裁員已經展開，預計於今年年底前完成。值得注意的是，公司特別指出其在美國的業務將不會受到此次裁員影響。

paragraphs.en: The Guardian reports that BAT is trying to build a future-ready organization that is more agile—meaning faster and more flexible—and disciplined with its spending. Chief executive Tadeu Marroco stated that the company is focused on supporting employees through this difficult transition with care and respect. These changes are part of a broader "transformation programme" designed to help the business adapt to a changing market.
paragraphs.zh: 《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，英美菸草公司正致力於打造一個具備未來競爭力的組織，使其更為敏捷（意即更快速、更靈活）並嚴格控管支出。執行長塔德烏·馬洛科（Tadeu Marroco）表示，公司將致力於在這一艱難的轉型過程中，以關懷與尊重的態度支持員工。這些變革是更廣泛的「轉型計畫」之一，旨在協助企業適應不斷變化的市場。

paragraphs.en: This announcement follows a difficult period for the tobacco industry. Traditional cigarette sales are shrinking globally as more smokers switch to alternative products like vapes and nicotine pouches. BAT is currently investing heavily in these "new categories," such as its Vuse vapes and Velo nicotine pouches, to drive future growth. While the company says revenue in these new areas is growing, the overall transition from cigarettes to these new products has been slow, according to Dan Coatsworth of AJ Bell.
paragraphs.zh: 這項公告發布於菸草產業的艱困時期。隨著越來越多吸菸者轉向電子菸和尼古丁袋等替代產品，傳統香菸的全球銷量正在萎縮。英美菸草公司目前正投入大量資金於這些「新類別」產品，例如其旗下的 Vuse 電子菸與 Velo 尼古丁袋，以推動未來成長。然而，根據 AJ Bell 的丹·科茨沃斯（Dan Coatsworth）分析，儘管公司表示這些新領域的營收正在成長，但從傳統香菸轉向這些新產品的整體過渡過程進展緩慢。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the shift in consumer habits, the company is facing several other challenges. The Guardian noted that BAT is dealing with falling demand for traditional cigarettes and is closing some of its older factories, such as a major site in South Africa. Additionally, the company is struggling with rising taxes and stricter government rules in many countries. In the United States, BAT has faced delays in getting approval for new products. The company claims that these delays have allowed illegal, low-cost products from China to enter the market, which has hurt its own sales and market share.
paragraphs.zh: 除了消費者習慣的轉變外，該公司還面臨其他多項挑戰。《衛報》指出，英美菸草公司正應對傳統香菸需求下降的問題，並正在關閉部分舊工廠，例如位於南非的一處大型廠區。此外，公司還在許多國家面臨稅收增加與政府法規趨嚴的困境。在美國，英美菸草公司在取得新產品核准時遭遇延宕。公司聲稱，這些延誤導致來自中國的非法低價產品得以進入市場，進而損害了自身的銷售額與市占率。

paragraphs.en: Economic factors are also playing a role. The BBC reported that in the United States, which is BAT’s largest market, the rising cost of living has caused many smokers to switch to cheaper, lower-quality brands. This pressure on household budgets has made it harder for the company to maintain its profit margins. To address these issues, BAT has been working with technology partners like Accenture to use advanced artificial intelligence (AI) solutions. By using AI, the company hopes to simplify its operations and become more efficient.
paragraphs.zh: 經濟因素也發揮了作用。BBC 報導指出，在英美菸草公司最大的市場美國，生活成本上升導致許多吸菸者轉而選擇更便宜、品質較差的品牌。這種家庭預算壓力使得公司難以維持利潤率。為了解決這些問題，英美菸草公司一直與埃森哲（Accenture）等科技合作夥伴攜手，運用先進的人工智慧（AI）解決方案。透過使用人工智慧，公司期望能簡化營運並提升效率。

paragraphs.en: Despite the scale of the job cuts, the company remains focused on its long-term goals. While shares in BAT fell slightly following the news, the company’s stock has generally performed well over the past year. Investors are watching closely to see if this new strategy will successfully turn the business around. As the company moves away from its traditional roots, the success of its smoke-free products will likely determine its future in a market that is becoming increasingly digital and strictly regulated.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管裁員規模龐大，該公司仍專注於其長期目標。雖然英美菸草公司的股價在消息公布後略有下跌，但該公司股票在過去一年整體表現良好。投資人正密切關注這項新策略是否能成功扭轉業務。隨著公司逐漸脫離傳統根基，其無菸產品的成敗，極可能決定其在一個日益數位化且監管嚴格的市場中的未來。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An action or step taken to achieve a specific goal.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（移動），這裡作名詞用。文中：This move represents nearly one-fifth of its total global workforce of 47,000 people.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause something to progress or increase.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（駕駛），這裡指推動業務成長。文中：BAT is currently investing heavily in these "new categories," such as its Vuse vapes and Velo nicotine pouches, to drive future growth.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause a business or situation to become successful after a period of failure.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 使（公司或情況）好轉、扭轉頹勢。
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞與副詞組成的片語，意思與字面拆解不同。文中：Investors are watching closely to see if this new strategy will successfully turn the business around.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: British American Tobacco to cut 9,000 jobs

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: British American Tobacco to slash 9,000 jobs as it turns to AI

previousArticles.title: British American Tobacco to Cut 9,000 Jobs in Major AI Shift
previousArticles.summaryEn: British American Tobacco is cutting 9,000 jobs to reduce costs and focus on new technology. The company is moving away from traditional cigarettes toward products like vapes and nicotine pouches. These changes are part of a plan to save £600 million annually by 2028. While the company faces challenges like illegal products and falling sales, it hopes to become more digital. These job losses will not affect the company's business in the United States.

quiz.question: What is the specific breakdown of the 9,000 job reductions planned by BAT?

options.A: 4,500 jobs will be cut in the US and 4,500 globally.
options.B: 5,500 roles will be eliminated and 3,500 will be outsourced.
options.C: 9,000 roles will be outsourced to technology partners like Accenture.
options.D: 3,500 roles will be eliminated and 5,500 will be moved to South Africa.

quiz.explanationZh: 根據文章第二段，BAT 計劃裁減的 9,000 個職位中，有 5,500 個是直接刪除（removing），另外 3,500 個則是外包（outsourcing）給外部公司。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到美國市場不會受到裁員影響；選項 C 錯誤，因為外包僅佔其中的 3,500 個職位，而非全部；選項 D 則是搞混了數字與地點的資訊。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might BAT be struggling to maintain its profit margins in the United States?

options.A: Because the company has stopped selling traditional cigarettes entirely in that market.
options.B: Because the company is focusing exclusively on high-cost, low-quality products.
options.C: Because consumers are choosing cheaper alternatives due to economic pressures.
options.D: Because the company has decided to outsource all its US operations to China.

quiz.explanationZh: 根據第六段，文章提到在美國市場，生活成本上升導致吸菸者轉向購買更便宜、品質較低的品牌，這對公司的利潤率造成了壓力。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章並未說停止銷售；選項 B 錯誤，消費者轉向低價產品並非公司主動選擇生產低品質產品；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到的是非法產品從中國進入市場，而非公司將業務外包至中國。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the word 'agile' mean in the context of the company's transformation?

options.A: Faster and more flexible in its operations.
options.C: Strictly focused on reducing the number of employees.
options.D: Capable of producing only digital technology products.

quiz.explanationZh: 文章第三段明確定義了 'agile' 的含義，即 'meaning faster and more flexible'（意指更快速且更靈活）。這是一個商業術語，形容企業在面對市場變化時的反應能力。選項 B 是該詞的字面意義，但在文中不適用；選項 C 與 D 雖然提到公司正在進行的變革，但並非 'agile' 一詞的直接定義。

quiz.question: What is the primary message regarding BAT's recent business strategy?

options.A: BAT is planning to exit the tobacco industry and move entirely into AI software.
options.B: The company is cutting jobs primarily because it has failed to make any profit in the last year.
options.C: BAT is restructuring and shifting toward new product categories to survive in a changing market.
options.D: The company's main goal is to close all factories in South Africa to save costs.

quiz.explanationZh: 全文的核心在於 BAT 正面臨傳統香菸銷量下滑的挑戰，因此透過裁員、數位轉型以及投資電子菸（vapes）和尼古丁袋（nicotine pouches）等新產品來轉型。選項 A 錯誤，公司並未退出菸草業；選項 B 錯誤，文章提到公司股價表現尚可；選項 D 錯誤，關閉南非工廠只是眾多挑戰中的一個細節，並非公司轉型的核心主旨。
